headline: Survey at 29% completion with strong regional spread but uneven stakeholder capture.
collection_status: 393 of 1,350 responses collected (29% of target). Coverage spans all 10 regions with Lindi, Mara, and Tanga driving volume. Pace suggests 957 responses still needed to meet target by survey close.
key_findings: Teacher engagement dominates: 207 of 393 respondents (53%) are KiuFunza-trained teachers, skewing sample toward direct implementers over decision-makers.
District/regional leadership severely underrepresented: only 17 responses (4%) from DEO, REO, ARC, REC combined—critical for policy and scale validation.
Expansion appetite is clear: 139 respondents (35%) prioritise expanding KiuFunza; only 8 (2%) flag research needs, indicating stakeholder confidence in model readiness.
Partner satisfaction moderately positive (4.4/5) but scale readiness weak (2.7/5)—gap suggests stakeholders see value but question operational capacity to expand.
flags: Geographic imbalance: Rukwa (9 responses, 2%) and Songwe (29) lag severely; risk of unrepresentative policy conclusions if southern/western regions remain undersampled.
Leadership voice missing: WEO, DEO, REO, ARC undersampled relative to survey's apparent policy/scale intent; current data reflects teacher sentiment, not institutional readiness.
Scale readiness crisis: 2.7/5 score contradicts expansion push—urgent diagnostic needed on what blocks readiness before presenting expansion recommendations to government.
recommendation: Immediately prioritise district and regional official outreach (target: 80+ responses from DEO, REO, ARC, REC tiers) and boost southern regions to 15% of total sample minimum. Simultaneously conduct rapid diagnostic interviews with 10-15 low-readiness respondents to isolate blocking factors before finalising scale recommendations.
